description_long: This is a wooden staircase ladder with a straight main section and a lower segment featuring two triangular, turning steps, likely designed for tight spaces or loft access. The ladder is constructed from light-colored wood, with visible grain and small round fasteners along the side rails. The steps are flat and evenly spaced, with the lower two steps forming a quarter-turn, allowing for a change in direction at the base. The side rails are thick and rectangular, providing structural support and stability. There are no visible handrails or additional safety features, and the overall design is utilitarian and robust. The finish appears matte, with a slightly rough texture typical of unfinished or lightly sanded wood.
description: A light wood staircase ladder with a straight run and two turning steps at the base, featuring rectangular side rails and flat, evenly spaced steps.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the full length of the ladder, the turning steps at the base, and the rectangular side rails.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the width of the steps and the triangular turning steps at the bottom.
description_view_2: Opposite angled view showing the straight run of steps and the side profile of the lower turning section.
description_view_3: Direct top-down view displaying the full set of steps and the quarter-turn at the base.
